# CEAS Mentors Duo Reclaims Gold, Overpowers CBA in Mixed Doubles Badminton Clash
[Image: CEAS Mentors Duo Reclaims Gold, Overpowers CBA in Mixed Doubles Badminton Clash]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**
TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—In a smashing display of speed, precision, and teamwork, the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors reclaimed badminton glory, dominating the mixed doubles championship against the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) Wizards on September 16, 2025, at the Trickshot Badminton Court.
Right from t

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash]
 and Accountancy (CBA) Wizards on September 16, 2025, at the Trickshot Badminton Court.
Right from the opening serve, the dynamic duo of Daniel Ruzzel Bernal and Mary Anne Lafuente wasted no time asserting their dominance with lightning-quick reflexes and razor-sharp shot placements.
Consequently, their rapid-fire rallies and clever net play kept the CBA Wizards scrambling across the court, ending the first set in convincing fashion at 21-9, with CEAS clearly in control.
Building on that first-set momentum, Bernal and Lafuente came out swinging in the second set, smashing their way to an early

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-duo-reclaims-gold-overpowers-cba-in-mixed-doubles-badminton-clash]
et momentum, Bernal and Lafuente came out swinging in the second set, smashing their way to an early lead through seamless communication and smart positioning.
Meanwhile, although the Wizards attempted a comeback with aggressive clears and pushes, every move was countered by the Mentors’ calculated drop shots, steady defense, and powerful smashes, closing the set at 21-1 to secure the championship crown.
After the final rally, Lafuente emphasized that the victory was not only a triumph of skill but also a testament to their trust in each other, both on and off the court.
Similarly, Bernal expr

but also a testament to their trust in each other, both on and off the court.
Similarly, Bernal expressed his pride and excitement after clinching the gold, saying, “Sobrang saya po and super proud po ako sa partner ko.”
Before reaching the finals, the duo battled past the CHTM Sharks in the elimination round, overcoming sharp smashes and relentless returns to secure a 30–10 win and a spot in the championship stage.
Furthermore, the CEAS pair showed that age and experience were no barriers, as their first match as college students turned into a golden opportunity to prove their fire and determ

# GC Welcomes Konyang University of South Korea
[Image: GC Welcomes Konyang University of South Korea]
[IPDEA](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ipdea/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, May 14, 2025**
On May 14, 2025, Gordon College warmly welcomed delegates from Konyang University, South Korea, as part of an initiative to explore academic collaboration between the two institutions. The delegation was composed of Ms. Youngmi Sohn, Head of the Global Education Innovation Headquarters, and Mr. Dong-Yeon Ko, Team Leader.
The visit aimed to strengthen internati

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-welcomes-konyang-university-of-south-korea]
 Innovation Headquarters, and Mr. Dong-Yeon Ko, Team Leader.
The visit aimed to strengthen international ties, particularly in the field of Allied Health Studies, and included a tour of the college’s facilities—classrooms, laboratories, and library—especially in relation to Konyang’s interest in developing a short-term language program.
During the meeting, the delegates expressed their interest in discussing various opportunities for collaboration and confirmed that they are currently reviewing the proposed Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Both institutions expressed optimism for a sustainable a

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-welcomes-konyang-university-of-south-korea]
the proposed Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Both institutions expressed optimism for a sustainable and meaningful partnership that supports global academic engagement and student development.

# Clean As You Go & Bring Your Own Trash Policy
[Image: Clean As You Go & Bring Your Own Trash Policy]
**Friday, August 8, 2025**
Instructions for Gordon College Students: Clean As You Go & Bring Your Own Trash Policy
Purpose:
To foster a clean and respectful campus environment while promoting sustainability through personal responsibility for waste.
Before Class or Activity:
1. Prepare Your Materials:
– Bring Your Own Trash: Carry a small, reusable bag or container to collect any waste you generate during class or activities. This could be a sturdy tote, a lunch bag, or any suitable container

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy]
rate during class or activities. This could be a sturdy tote, a lunch bag, or any suitable container.
– Plan for Snacks/Drinks: If you plan to consume food or beverages, pack them in a way that minimizes waste (e.g., reusable containers, bottles).
During Class or Activity:
2. Maintain Your Space:
– Keep Your Area Clean: Throughout the session, stay vigilant about your surroundings. Avoid leaving papers, wrappers, or any personal items strewn about.
– Immediate Clean-Up: If you create a mess (accidental spills, paper cuts, etc.), address it promptly. If cleaning supplies are available, use them

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy]
idental spills, paper cuts, etc.), address it promptly. If cleaning supplies are available, use them to tidy up immediately.
3. Responsible Waste Management:
– Use Your Reusable Container: Whenever you have waste, place it in your reusable bag or container. Keep it nearby for easy access.
– Respect Shared Spaces: Be mindful of not just your space but also those around you. Dispose of any common area waste (e.g., napkins from community snacks) in your own trash.
After Class or Activity:
4. Final Cleanup:
– Inspect Your Area:
Before leaving, take a moment to ensure your seating area and any comm

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy]
Cleanup:
– Inspect Your Area:
Before leaving, take a moment to ensure your seating area and any communal spaces (tables, chairs) are tidy.
– Empty Your Trash into Appropriate Bins: When you leave the classroom or activity, empty your reusable container at designated trash or recycling bins outside the classroom (if available). Take anything that belongs to you with you.
5. Dispose Responsibly:
– Recycle When Possible: If your waste is recyclable, place it in the recycling bin. Be sure to check the college’s recycling guidelines to ensure proper disposal.
Additional Responsibilities:
6. Encoura

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy]
he college’s recycling guidelines to ensure proper disposal.
Additional Responsibilities:
6. Encourage a Culture of Cleanliness:
– Remind Peers:If you notice a classmate leaving a mess, gently remind them or offer to help.
– Lead by Example: Demonstrating your commitment to the “Clean As You Go” policy can inspire your peers to do the same.
7. Feedback and Improvement:
– Participate in discussions or feedback sessions organized by the college to share your insights on how the Clean As You Go policy is working.
8. Engage with Initiatives:
– Stay updated on any college-led environmental initiati

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/clean-as-you-go-bring-your-own-trash-policy]
icy is working.
8. Engage with Initiatives:
– Stay updated on any college-led environmental initiatives or clean-up days, and participate actively when you can.
By adhering to these instructions, you contribute to creating a cleaner, more sustainable campus environment while developing valuable habits of personal responsibility and community stewardship. Thank you for doing your part!

# Fostering Mental Health
[Image: Fostering Mental Health]
[Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/extension/) [RDCES](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/rdces/) [Community Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/community-extension/)
**Wednesday, July 26, 2023**
In adherence to our commitment to community service, the GC Community Extension Services Unit (GC-CESU), in collaboration with the GC Guidance Office and Tapinac Senior High School, successfully organized and conducted a seminar on July 31, 2023. The seminar aimed to enhance the resilience of the attendees by equi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/fostering-mental-health]
ted a seminar on July 31, 2023. The seminar aimed to enhance the resilience of the attendees by equipping them with techniques to address and manage challenges, emphasizing the importance of self-care, addressing mental health stigma, and facilitating access to mental health services. Ms. Mechel Bautista, GC Guidance Office Head, served as the resource speaker. The seminar saw active participation from 42 teachers from Tapinac Senior High School and took place at the Gordon College function hall.

# GC Color Run Kicks Off 27th Founding Anniversary
[Image: GC Color Run Kicks Off 27th Founding Anniversary]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, February 22, 2026**
The annual GC FUNdation RUN marked the start of Gordon College’s 27th Founding Anniversary, held on February 22, 2026, at the East Tapinac Oval Track.
The event gathered members of the Gordon College Faculty Association (GCFAS), administrative staff, students, alumni, and community participants under the pre-foundation day celebration motif, “Color Run.”
The program commenced with the assembly of partic

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-color-run-kicks-off-27th-founding-anniversary]
pre-foundation day celebration motif, “Color Run.”
The program commenced with the assembly of participants, followed by an opening ceremony that featured welcome messages from Dr. Katherine Yap and Dr. Imelda D.P. Soriano. A Zumba warm-up session, led by administrators, faculty members, and students from the Bachelor of Physical Education (BPED), energized attendees ahead of the race.
The run officially started at 6:30 AM as participants filled the track dressed in white shirts and athletic attire, ready to be splashed with vibrant colored powder and water.
More than a display of color and mov

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-color-run-kicks-off-27th-founding-anniversary]
re, ready to be splashed with vibrant colored powder and water.
More than a display of color and movement, this year’s FUNdation RUN is a colorful display of the institution’s commitment to promoting wellness, unity, and community relations while marking the upcoming foundation celebration with lively and memorable moments. | via Sam Andrea R. Delos Reyes/The FOREFRONT

# WARRIORS’ CARNAGE: GC Bags Second Championship Streak at LCUAA National Games
[Image: WARRIORS’ CARNAGE: GC Bags Second Championship Streak at LCUAA National Games]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, October 25, 2025**
After six days of grueling competition, the Gordon College (GC) Warriors are hailed as the ultimate victors of the 14th Local Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (LCUAA) National Games, marking GC’s second winning streak as the overall champion.
Across several categories, the Warriors collectively bagged 31 gold, 22 silver, and 24 bro

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/warriors-carnage-gc-bags-second-championship-streak-at-lcuaa-national-games]
champion.
Across several categories, the Warriors collectively bagged 31 gold, 22 silver, and 24 bronze medals across their LCUAA stint, a resounding sweep above overall 2nd place Kolehiyo ng Lungsod ng Lipa’s (KLL) 23 gold, 18 silver, and 21 bronze medal tally.
Meanwhile, the overall 3rd place title was claimed by Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Pasig (PLP) with 15 gold, 13 silver, and 15 bronze medals across their run.
Furthermore, GC brought home championship plaques in the swimming, dancesport, and taekwondo categories awarded today, October 25, at the CSJDM Sports Complex. The Olongapo-reps were

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/warriors-carnage-gc-bags-second-championship-streak-at-lcuaa-national-games]
 taekwondo categories awarded today, October 25, at the CSJDM Sports Complex. The Olongapo-reps were also awarded plaques for 1st runner-up in badminton, chess, and men’s futsal, as well as; 2nd runner-up in athletics.
The Warriors are set to defend their winning streak at next year’s national games at Binoloan, Pangasinan, hosted by the University of Eastern Pangasinan (UEP). | via Lance Isaac Leon/The FOREFRONT

# Gordon College Celebrates World Teachers’ Day
[Image: Gordon College Celebrates World Teachers’ Day]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, October 7, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE MAIN BLDG—The Office of the Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) hosted a World Teachers’ Day Celebration, together with various student organizations, which aims to honor the dedicated instructors of the institution on October 6, 2025, at Gordon College Main Bldg—Function Hall.
The event began with an opening prayer and remarks from Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Gordon College President, and Dr. Darwi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
h an opening prayer and remarks from Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Gordon College President, and Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs.
“We cannot have a lavish lifestyle in teaching. But your dedication and passion will definitely [inaudible] through saying our students’ success— changing their lives, especially those who are underprivileged students, and you are making them a story,” said Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, showing her gratitude for the effort of Gordon College’s faculty.
Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also expressed to the faculty that the celebration isn’t just about their perseve

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
. Darwin P. Paguio also expressed to the faculty that the celebration isn’t just about their perseverance, but also to take care of all the hard work in the classrooms as they invest in nurturing curiosity that will nourish them for a lifetime.
After the opening ceremony, Performing Arts Students Ensemble (PASE) Voci tributed a chorale performance that honors the educators.
To spark joy with the event, the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Student Council, Midwifery Student Organization (MSO), and the Nursing Student Organization (NSO) hosted games to energize and entertain the teachers.

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
SO), and the Nursing Student Organization (NSO) hosted games to energize and entertain the teachers.
Furthermore, the PASE Ballo sparked warmth and embrace with a performance number.
Moreover, the Supreme Student Council (SSC) and Human Resource Management Unit (HRMU) conducted an awarding ceremony to appreciate the faculty’s skills with admiration and humor.
Meanwhile, Hon. Rolen Paulino Jr., City Mayor of Olongapo, held a speech and cash raffle for the faculty.
“Salamat sa sacrifices niyo, sa mahabang pasensiya ninyo, tiyaka sa lahat ng ginagawa niyo para sa mga students natin,” Paulino stat

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
mahabang pasensiya ninyo, tiyaka sa lahat ng ginagawa niyo para sa mga students natin,” Paulino stated.
An Audio-Visual Presentation (AVP) video was shown to the faculty, featuring greetings and messages from the students, followed by the students who presented gifts to their beloved tutors.
Lastly, another raffle event was held with prizes for the faculty, which filled the room with joy and laughter.
The said event was supposed to be celebrated on October 3rd but was rescheduled due to Typhoon Paolo.| via Jasmine Zeah Llagas/The FOREFRONT

# 𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧
[Image: 𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧]
**Thursday, December 11, 2025**
𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓
𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧
To all 𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐞𝐬, please be advised that the claiming of your financial grants will be held at 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞, 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐇𝐚𝐥𝐥 (𝟑𝐫𝐝 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐨𝐫) 𝐨𝐧 𝐃𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝟏𝟏–𝟏𝟐, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓, 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝟕:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌 𝐭𝐨 𝟓:𝟎𝟎 𝐏𝐌.
This payout covers the following:
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟕.𝟏 – 𝟐𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟑–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟑 –

payout covers the following:
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟕.𝟏 – 𝟐𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟑–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟑 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓
• 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐋𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐧 𝟏𝟓 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓

Please prepare the following complete documents :
1. 𝐓𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐭𝐨𝐜𝐨𝐩𝐢𝐞𝐬 of your 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞), each with 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬
2. 𝐅𝐨𝐫 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐝 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐬 (𝐈𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐅𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐎𝐍𝐋𝐘) — all in 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐜𝐨𝐩𝐢𝐞𝐬:
a. Original 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐋𝐞𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐫 with the grantee’s wet signature.
b. Photocopy of the 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞

b. Photocopy of the 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 (with signature) of the grantee, each with 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬.

2. Bring 𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐬𝐞𝐭 of required documents 𝐩𝐞𝐫 𝐬𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫. If you’re included in 𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐡 𝐬𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐬, prepare 𝐭𝐰𝐨 (𝟐) 𝐬𝐞𝐭𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐞𝐚𝐜𝐡 𝐝𝐨𝐜𝐮𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬.
3. This is 𝐍𝐎𝐓 for application.

# GC Welcomes 5 new Registered Midwifes, Perfect passing in November MLE
[Image: GC Welcomes 5 new Registered Midwifes, Perfect passing in November MLE]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, November 19, 2025**
Five alumni from the College of Allied Health and Services (CAHS) of Gordon College (GC) passed the Midwives Licensure Exam (MLE) according to the Professional Regulation Commission Board.
GC achieved a perfect passing rate of 100% with 5 passers out of 5 examinees, once again surpassing the rate of last year’s results of 93.48% and the national rate of 57.

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-welcomes-5-new-registered-midwifes-perfect-passing-in-november-mle]
minees, once again surpassing the rate of last year’s results of 93.48% and the national rate of 57.60%. They constitute the 2,152 newly registered midwives.
All are first-time takers of this year’s exam.
Additionally, Tagaloan Community College is the top-performing school in the licensure exam with a perfect passing rate from 85 examinees of the school.
Meanwhile, Princesa Dumale Talamayan, this year’s board topnotcher, from Palawan State University achieved a 94.00%.
The licensure exam was held on November 8-9 and 16. | via Roi Chester B. Manio/The FOREFRONT

[Image: 𝐈𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐍𝐨𝐰 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐧 𝐢𝐧 𝐂𝐚𝐦𝐩𝐮𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐒𝐎𝐆𝐈𝐄-𝐈𝐧𝐜𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐔𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐏𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, August 5, 2025**
The Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) released an advisory on July 31, 2025, regarding the implementation of a new uniform policy that formally recognizes the right of students to wear components of the existing prescribed uniform that align with their Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, and Sex Characteristics (SOGIESC).
Th

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2]
 their Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, and Sex Characteristics (SOGIESC).
This initiative aims to provide a space for members of the LGBTQIA+ community to express their authentic selves freely within the campus.
According to the advisory, the implementation of the proper wearing of uniforms, including this new provision, will start on September 8, 2025, for freshmen and transferees.
Meanwhile, for continuing students, the change will take effect on August 18, 2025. The wearing of the prescribed uniform will remain compulsory for all students from Monday to Thursday.
“In

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2]
aring of the prescribed uniform will remain compulsory for all students from Monday to Thursday.
“In recognition of the spectrum of gender identities within our student body, the institution affirms the right of individuals to express their authentic selves through their attire,” the institution affirmed in its official statement.
Furthermore, on “wash days” (Friday, Saturday, Sunday, and Mid-Year Term), students have the right to choose their attire, as long as it adheres to the College’s prescribed school attire and maintains decency.
This means the students are now allowed to choose from th

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%9d%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ad%f0%9d%90%b2-%f0%9d%90%8d%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%b0-%f0%9d%90%96%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a7-%f0%9d%90%a2]
ribed school attire and maintains decency.
This means the students are now allowed to choose from the established guidelines for male and female clothing, specifically, the pants or skirts that are already part of the school’s approved uniform, to express their gender identity best, provided the chosen attire still adheres to the College’s overall dress code and decency standards.
The said office encourages the entire Gordon College community to ensure the implementation of this new policy.

# LIMC History
Gordon College Library started in 1999 when the former Olongapo City Colleges, now Gordon College, put up a curricular program for Computer Studies and Business Management and Accountancy. With only a few books and mostly computer subjects in hand, Mrs. Remedios Sapuan, who was then a Utility worker, took charge of the library operation with the assistance of the Administrative Staff.
The Library moved four times to different locations in the building and its fourth was in the former KHG Hall. When the library was first opened, books were in the cabinet with glass doors and were

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
mer KHG Hall. When the library was first opened, books were in the cabinet with glass doors and were locked. The library was using the close shelf system, where stacks cannot be directly accessed by users. The students should approach the assigned staff when borrowing books and it was then the bookshelf was opened to get the book needed.
In 2002, Thelma Ramos, a clerk aid of the Assessor’s Office, was transferred to the College Library to assist Mrs. Sapuan in the library operation. According to Mrs. Sapuan, a certain instructor, in the person of Mr. Sydney Maglalang, was temporarily assigned

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
 Mrs. Sapuan, a certain instructor, in the person of Mr. Sydney Maglalang, was temporarily assigned to the library. Services of the staff in the City Library were borrowed in the processing of books acquired for the College Library. In June 2003, the first College Librarian was hired, Ms. Maria Corazon D. Maneja as a casual position. Later she was promoted to a permanent position. The problem was by her was the book organization, the right classification of books, classification tools (DDC) Dewey Decimal Classification System and there were no catalog cards and cabinets. Some of the books were

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
y Decimal Classification System and there were no catalog cards and cabinets. Some of the books were infested by termites which were already problems before but were not treated completely. Books were thenr eorganized, classified, and cataloged and were assigned new numbers using DD Classification tools that were borrowed from the Ci4ty Library. The circulation counter and grills dividing the books stacks and reading area were removed. Shelves were distributed and fastened on all library walls to make the books accessible to the users and to widen the reading area. Heavily termite-infested boo

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
o make the books accessible to the users and to widen the reading area. Heavily termite-infested books were removed and deleted from the records. New books were formed. Newspapers were compiled with the date labeled and put on display. In 2006, books from Australia were received by the library through the Rotary Club of Olongapo. Books were found to be outdated and mostly for High School and Elementary level students. In the same year, another book donation was received from Asia Foundations, Inc. Books became very useful to patrons because of their general content. In March 2007, Ms. Betty Fi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
c. Books became very useful to patrons because of their general content. In March 2007, Ms. Betty Fielder, a friend of Mrs. Dely Aristorenas, former College Administrator, donated books and magazines for use by HRM students and faculty. Two air conditioners were installed to replace the former electric fans for ventilation and besides its small space, library patrons flocked the library especially nursing students who occupied the most number of enrollees. The library had then 8,361 volumes of books as of October 2007.
In the summer of 2010, the librarian during one of the EXECOM (ExecutiveCom

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
ooks as of October 2007.
In the summer of 2010, the librarian during one of the EXECOM (ExecutiveCommittee) meetings, suggested removing over duplicated copies of books to provide spaces for new arrivals. Enough copies from weeded books were retained. 2,958 books were discarded and returned to GSO (Government Supply Office) but the latter suggested just donating them to the City Library.
In 2012, Gordon College's 13th Founding Anniversary, a Give-a-Book Project was launched and spearheaded by the College Library to collect tertiary level books from students, faculty, alumni, and friends of

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
y the College Library to collect tertiary level books from students, faculty, alumni, and friends of Gordon College. As of this date, May 6, 2013, we have collected 182 book donations.
On August 7, 2012, when the first knee-deep flood entered the College Library, books located on the first two-row level shelves were partially damaged but were restored. But on September 23, 2013, during a chest-deep heavy flood of Olongapo City, almost 2/3 of the library materials were soaked in the mud and water and devastated and discarded. Furniture which was made of light materials, files, and newly acquire

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
 and devastated and discarded. Furniture which was made of light materials, files, and newly acquired books was included, but other computers were restored. Due to the kindness of City Mayor Rolen C. Paulino, he approved a 1 million budget for the purchase of new general education books, professional books, and journals. The first set of books was delivered in December 2013. Another era was opened for Gordon College Library for its transfer to the new building funded and built by SM in 2015-2016. The official transfer began on June 24, 2016, until July of the same year. The new and much wider

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
. The official transfer began on June 24, 2016, until July of the same year. The new and much wider library which is located on the fourth floor was opened to students on August 1, 2016, the start of the first semester, the Academic Year 2016- 2017.
Mr. Marlon A. Atencio recommended to the Executive Council the change of the library name into “Library and Instructional Media Center” for the reason that the library is now having a media center that supports the educational needs of the library and it was approved by the Executive Council spearheaded by the College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Sori

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
d it was approved by the Executive Council spearheaded by the College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano last May 31, 2018.

# 𝐈𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨 𝐚𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐤𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐚𝐦𝐮𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐨, 𝐛𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐠𝐲𝐚𝐧 𝐧𝐠 𝐛𝐚𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐤𝐭𝐢𝐛𝐨
[Image: 𝐈𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨 𝐚𝐭 𝐈𝐧𝐤𝐥𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐚𝐦𝐮𝐦𝐮𝐧𝐨, 𝐛𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐠𝐲𝐚𝐧 𝐧𝐠 𝐛𝐚𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐤𝐭𝐢𝐛𝐨]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, July 18, 2025**
Pinaglaanan ng panibagong pananaw ang mga kasalukuyang student leader ng Dalubhasaan Gordon patungkol sa paksa ng pamumuno bilang lider sa katatapos na Capacity Building and Personality Development Program for Student Organizations 2025 na ginanap sa Function 1, SMX Olongapo City Convention Center.
Ang naturang seminar na may temang: “Leadership Rooted in Purp

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a]
 SMX Olongapo City Convention Center.
Ang naturang seminar na may temang: “Leadership Rooted in Purpose: Strengthening Self, Serving Others, Shaping Tomorrow” ay dinaluhan ng dalawang resource speaker mula Central Luzon State University (CLSU) na sina Dr. Irene G. Bustos, CLSU Student Affairs and Services Dean at ni Ms. Joan B. Pelagio, CLSU Paruparong Bukid Adviser.
Ang nabanggit na talakayan ay pinasimulan ni Dr. Bustos sa kanyang presentasyon tungkol sa kung paano nga ba ang tamang pamumuno ng isang lider sa kanyang nasasakupan at mga kalidad na dapat niyang tinataglay. Binigyang pansin din

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a]
g isang lider sa kanyang nasasakupan at mga kalidad na dapat niyang tinataglay. Binigyang pansin din ni Dr. Bustos kung paano gawing reyalidad ang bisyon ng isang lider.
Sa kabilang banda, binuksan naman ni Ms. Joan B. Pelagio, CLSU Paruparong Bukid Adviser, ang diskusyong hinggil sa pagiging gender-responsive at pagbuo ng isang inklusibong kapaligiran at sa loob ng isang institusyon.
Ayon kay Ms. Pelagio, sa pamamagitan ng gender-responsiveness at inklusibong pakikitungo, mas makakabuo tayo ng pagkakaisa, respeto, at ligtas kapaligiran.
“Nag-start kami with few so with support and respect na

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a]
pagkakaisa, respeto, at ligtas kapaligiran.
“Nag-start kami with few so with support and respect na hindi kami hinahayaan kung hindi sinusuportahan kami na gawin kung ano yung mga activities na kailangan, ‘yun yung isa sa nagmo-motivate sa group o sa org para ituloy yung advocacy.” Saad ni Ms. Pelagio ukol sa lagay ng Paruparong Bukid sa kanilang institusyon.
Si Ms. Pelagio ang tagapayo ng Paruparong Bukid, isang organisasyong nabuo sa isang obserbasyon at pagsasaliksik nina Dr. Bustos patungkol sa homophobia at transphobia na siyang naging pasimundan ng MAYARI na organisayon naman ng LGBTQIA+

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a]
 sa homophobia at transphobia na siyang naging pasimundan ng MAYARI na organisayon naman ng LGBTQIA+ ng Dalubhasaang Gordon.
Sa kabuuan ng nasabing seminar, “If you have passion for change, keep that passion fired, ika nga, [and] lighted, and pursue your vision and make it a reality. Be aware of everything that happens within and outside yourself. And kung anuman ang mga challenges, mga issues, problems ‘no parte lahat ito ng pagiging leader kaya hold on and keep the fire burning — yung passion mo to lead and to make change for the betterment of your studentry, the community, and siyempre the

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%88%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%a7%f0%9d%90%a0-%f0%9d%90%a9%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%a8%f0%9d%90%a0%f0%9d%90%ab%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%9b%f0%9d%90%a8-%f0%9d%90%9a]
mo to lead and to make change for the betterment of your studentry, the community, and siyempre the nation as a whole.” Ani Dr. Bustos sa mga kasalukuyang student leader at nagnanais na maging lider sa hinaharap.

# 𝐌𝐀𝐘𝐀𝐑𝐈 𝐑𝐢𝐬𝐞𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐥𝐚𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬 𝐟𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭 𝐋𝐆𝐁𝐓𝐐𝐈𝐀+ 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐎𝐫𝐠𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧
[Image: 𝐌𝐀𝐘𝐀𝐑𝐈 𝐑𝐢𝐬𝐞𝐬: 𝐆𝐂 𝐥𝐚𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬 𝐟𝐢𝐫𝐬𝐭 𝐋𝐆𝐁𝐓𝐐𝐈𝐀+ 𝐒𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐎𝐫𝐠𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, June 18, 2025**
Gordon College established its first LGBTQIA+ Student Organization named “Mga Anak ng Yugtong Apo para sa Respeto at Inklusyon (MAYARI),” in line with the celebration of Pride Month this June.
MAYARI aims to champion the rights and well-being of LGBTQIA+ students within the college and beyond.
Furthermore, Mr. Marko Aratea, organization adviser of MAYARI, confirmed that the orga

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae]
e and beyond.
Furthermore, Mr. Marko Aratea, organization adviser of MAYARI, confirmed that the organization has developed rapidly and has profound objectives.
“We, at MAYARI, are currently in the process of becoming a formally recognized civic organization at Gordon College, but we will get there very soon!” Mr. Aratea stated.
Moreover, MAYARI was formed with both students and the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) in mind as a dedicated community for LGBTQIA+ students and their allies. Both Mr. Aratea and co-adviser Mr. Christian Loid Valenzuela started as the founding members who

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae]
es. Both Mr. Aratea and co-adviser Mr. Christian Loid Valenzuela started as the founding members who led the creation of the constitution, set clear objectives, and met institutional standards.
The central aim of MAYARI is to establish a safe and inclusive space for LGBTQIA+ students. Mr. Aratea outlined the organization’s primary objectives: “MAYARI aims to provide a safe and inclusive space for LGBTQIA+ students and allies in our humble institution. Our main goals are to promote visibility, inclusivity, respect, empowerment, and solidarity.”
MAYARI intends to accomplish these objectives thro

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae]
clusivity, respect, empowerment, and solidarity.”
MAYARI intends to accomplish these objectives through education by providing LGBTQIA+ seminars, webinars, gender sensitivity programs, and opportunities for creativity and leadership.
Furthermore, one of the important goals of MAYARI is to handle issues affecting LGBTQIA+ individuals, mainly discrimination, mental health challenges, and lack of community understanding.
“MAYARI is a civic organization for LGBTQIA+ and allies in GC, also serving as a platform to address catalysts related to gender-based concerns through formal dialogues programs

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae]
 a platform to address catalysts related to gender-based concerns through formal dialogues programs and activities that will equip relevant stakeholders regarding said concerns and practical solutions,” MAYARI’s adviser affirmed.
Meanwhile, the organization will implement peer support initiatives, explore partnerships with the college’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) office, and launch extensive awareness campaigns on SOGIESC (Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, Gender Expression, and Sex Characteristics).
Looking beyond symbolic gestures, MAYARI envisions a future where “total accept

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/%f0%9d%90%8c%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%98%f0%9d%90%80%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%88-%f0%9d%90%91%f0%9d%90%a2%f0%9d%90%ac%f0%9d%90%9e%f0%9d%90%ac-%f0%9d%90%86%f0%9d%90%82-%f0%9d%90%a5%f0%9d%90%9a%f0%9d%90%ae]
ex Characteristics).
Looking beyond symbolic gestures, MAYARI envisions a future where “total acceptance” is deeply ingrained in the institution’s policies and culture.
“Total acceptance means more than symbolic gestures—it involves policy and culture change,” Mr. Aratea emphasized and further discussed that the organization aims to integrate inclusivity into the college’s systemic operations rather than limiting it to mere statements.

# CEAS Pins 269 Pre-service Educators in 18th Convocation Ceremony
[Image: CEAS Pins 269 Pre-service Educators in 18th Convocation Ceremony]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, December 10, 2025**
SMX Convention Center — The College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) marked a milestone in the journey of its future educators during the 18th Pinning and Candle Lighting Ceremony last December 3, Wednesday, as the student teachers became full fledged practice teachers.
This year’s cohort included a total of 269 student teachers across various programs: 22 from

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
chers.
This year’s cohort included a total of 269 student teachers across various programs: 22 from Bachelor of Culture and Arts Education (BCAEd), 17 from Bachelor of Early Childhood Education (BECED), 50 from Bachelor of Elementary Education (BEED), 42 from Bachelor of Physical Education (BPEd), and 138 from Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSEd) who are deployed in various public and private schools across Olongapo City.
In his welcome remarks, Dr. Darwin Paguio, OIC-Dean of the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), emphasized Gordon College’s commitment to transferring theory le

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
ucation, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS), emphasized Gordon College’s commitment to transferring theory learned in the classroom onto the field. He highlighted how CEAS’s education programs ensure that every deployed student teacher is academically prepared within the “four corners of the classroom” and fully ready to apply these theories once they step into real teaching environments.
Following this is the ceremonial passing of the Torch and Book of Wisdom, carried by Mr. Kristoffer Obispo and Ms. Juanalyn Olaguer, respectively. “The torch has been an important symbol since early civilization,” sai

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
analyn Olaguer, respectively. “The torch has been an important symbol since early civilization,” said Dr. Catherine Mobo as the torch is lit, explaining that it embodies the “light of tomorrow” that educators carry into the lives of learners. Meanwhile, Ms. Agnes Melo presented the Book of Wisdom, reminding the future educators that “its pages hold not only theories but the hopes and futures of learners we are called to guide.”
Delivering the keynote speech, Ms. Ilynne Samonte, CESE, Assistant Schools Division Superintendent, shared five heartfelt “pabaon” from her own internship journey at Ph

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
chools Division Superintendent, shared five heartfelt “pabaon” from her own internship journey at Philippine Normal University (PNU). She shared lessons she hopes the student teachers will carry into their practicum from the significance of being on time to the importance of taking care of oneself.
She also reminded the attendees of their future roles in public service, expressing her confidence that many of the candidates would one day serve in the Department of Education: “Most of you, if not all of you, will join us at DepEd sooner or later… Sabi nga, hindi pwede ang ‘pwede na.’ Ultimately,

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
all of you, will join us at DepEd sooner or later… Sabi nga, hindi pwede ang ‘pwede na.’ Ultimately, that will be your contribution to your community and your country.”
The 269 student teachers were ceremoniously pinned by their guardians with an official teacher’s nameplate. Their designated resource teachers — tenured teachers in their deployed school who have guided them — also passed the flames of their candle onto the wicks of their student teacher’s candle.
Solemn tears were shed at SMX as an audio-visual presentation was shown with photos from their college journey set to Ben & Ben’s hi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
 an audio-visual presentation was shown with photos from their college journey set to Ben & Ben’s hit song “Saranggola”. “Sa’n man tayo hipan ng amihan, hindi ipagpapalit ang pagkakaibigan,” the song played as numerous iconic moments through the years flashed on screen.
Emotions further deepened as the student teachers sang a musical tribute of “MAPA” by SB19 as they gifted a rose to their parents whose support made the milestone possible.
The ceremony concluded with closing remarks from Mr. Samuel Felip D. Fastidio, Field Study Supervisor and Program Chair of the BPEd Program, who congratulat

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
uel Felip D. Fastidio, Field Study Supervisor and Program Chair of the BPEd Program, who congratulated the candidates and encouraged them to carry their symbols—torch, book, and candle—with a purpose as they step into the teaching profession as Gordon College’s newest teaching interns.
After hours of classroom observation in their field study, the 269 student teachers who are deployed in various schools will now formally step into their practicum as they take over their respective school classrooms. | via Love Lorraine Acedo & Lance Isaac Leon, The FOREFRONT
Photos by Jasmine Shameer Bañaga
CT

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-pins-269-pre-service-educators-in-18th-convocation-ceremony]
oms. | via Love Lorraine Acedo & Lance Isaac Leon, The FOREFRONT

# GC marks a milestone in ALCUCOA 2025
[Image: GC marks a milestone in ALCUCOA 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, December 3, 2025**
The three-day program comes to an end as Gordon College (GC) conducts an exit conference for the Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) last November 28, at the Gordon College Main Building, Function Hall.
Vying for one Level III program, five Level II programs, and two candidate programs this year, the institution’s big step is a leap for development, embodying character, excell

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-a-milestone-in-alcucoa-2025]
rograms this year, the institution’s big step is a leap for development, embodying character, excellence, and service along the way.
“This is not the end,” Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano opened the exit conference of this year’s accreditation with a firm statement.
The accreditors commended the institution’s strengths including the Library Ambassadors Organization (LAO), community extension programs, and professionalism.
Meanwhile, they also recommended improvements such as stronger programs to supplement both students’ and teachers’ needs, more tangible and sustainable community extensions, and subje

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-a-milestone-in-alcucoa-2025]
nt both students’ and teachers’ needs, more tangible and sustainable community extensions, and subjects that focus on application rather than theories.
As Dr. Elizabeth Montero, the supervising accreditor said in her message, “The new oil now is consistency,” a reminder to stay true and stand into the lenses of continuous development where growth is evident and seen.
In response, Ms. Geraldine Raydanas and Mrs. Rowenalyn Sagun expressed their utmost gratitude and promised to improve in the areas mentioned.
Ending the program, Dr. Kristin Joy Mendoza, secretary of GC’s Board of Trustees, left a

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-a-milestone-in-alcucoa-2025]
 mentioned.
Ending the program, Dr. Kristin Joy Mendoza, secretary of GC’s Board of Trustees, left a valuable statement regarding institutionalization and sustainability.
“Every accreditation is an opportunity to innovate and fill the spaces where we can do better,” Dr. Mendoza emphasized.
Meanwhile, the results of the 2025 accreditation are set to be announced in the next few weeks. | via Mikhaela Sagun/The FOREFRONT

# PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation
[Image: PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, August 29, 2025**
Gordon College warmly welcomed the Public Employment Service Office – Tarlac Center for Learning and Skills Success (PESO-TCLASS) under the Provincial Government of Tarlac last August 26, 2025, for a benchmarking visit.
As part of their preparations to offer the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program and apply for a Certificate of

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation]
reparations to offer the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program and apply for a Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC), the PESO-TCLASS delegation, headed by Dr. Maria Lira Reyes – PESO Manager and School Administrator, engaged with Gordon College to learn from our best practices in curriculum development, faculty qualifications, library and physical facilities, equipment, support services, and quality assurance systems. The visit provided them with valuable insights and guidance on meeting the minimum standards required for program compliance.
This knowledge-sharing initiative reflect

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation]
ing the minimum standards required for program compliance.
This knowledge-sharing initiative reflects Gordon College’s continued commitment to collaboration and capacity-building among academic institutions.

# GCAAI Unveils New Student Lounge at Gordon College
[Image: GCAAI Unveils New Student Lounge at Gordon College]
[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, August 15, 2025**
GCAAI Unveils New Student Lounge at Gordon College
The Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. (GCAAI) is proud to announce the official blessing and turnover of the new Gordon College Student Lounge. The lounge, a project lovingly referred to as the “Garden Project,” was blessed by Rev. Fr. Mike Duffin before being officially handed over to the co

1. Go to the Gordon College Admission Portal
2. Click “Sign up”
3. Fill in:

3. Fill in: email, given name, last name, date of birth, contact number, desired password, and type of student (New, Transferee, Second Courser)
[Image: 📌]
Notes:
• Use a valid and existing email address.
• Make sure all details are correct.
• DO NOT create multiple accounts (ground for disapproval).
• Remember your email and password.

1. Upload scanned copies or clear photos of the required documents (≤ 2MB each):
o Profile photo
– 2×2 colored picture, white background
– Formal pose, collared top
– No eyeglasses or accessories
– Taken within 6 months before filing
o Good Moral Character Certificate
o PSA Authenticated Birth Certificate
o Senior High School ID or Valid ID
o Academic Records (Grade 11 grades for SHS, TOR for transferees)
2. Click “Continue with Sign Up” to finalize.
Important: Always double-check to ensure that all documents have been successfully uploaded.

# GC marks second year in One Billion Rising movement to end violence against women and girls
[Image: GC marks second year in One Billion Rising movement to end violence against women and girls]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, February 16, 2026**
Gordon College marked its second consecutive year of participation in the global campaign One Billion Rising through a public awareness activity themed “Rise for our bodies, our Earth, our Future,” held at Ayala Malls Harbor Point on February 14.  
The event brought together students and mall-goers in a collective call

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
rbor Point on February 14.  
The event brought together students and mall-goers in a collective call to stand against violence toward women and girls while promoting empowerment and social responsibility.  
Established in 2012, One Billion Rising has developed into an international movement recognized for mobilizing communities across the world to advocate for the elimination of gender-based violence.  
The initiative is grounded in global data from the United Nations indicating that approximately one in three women experience violence at some point in their lives, representing more than a bil

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
ly one in three women experience violence at some point in their lives, representing more than a billion women and girls worldwide.  
The activity was led by the Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs (IPDEA) through the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Unit in partnership with the National Service Training Program (NSTP) and Ayala Malls Inc., with the goal of strengthening public awareness and encouraging community participation in the advocacy.  
One of the program’s highlights was a flash mob performance by Pase Ballo and SHAPES, who led a mass dance to the One Bill

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
ighlights was a flash mob performance by Pase Ballo and SHAPES, who led a mass dance to the One Billion Rising anthem, Break the Chain, symbolizing unity and resistance.  
Mr. Joseph Atencio, head of the DEI Unit, emphasized that violence against women and girls does not only occur in public spaces but is often present within homes and personal relationships.  
“Bukod sa awareness na gusto nating ipakalat, gusto nating maging mapagmatyag ‘yung mga tao. The purpose of One Billion Rising is truly to end violence against women and girls. There can be no sustainable development without women’s emp

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
to end violence against women and girls. There can be no sustainable development without women’s empowerment,” he remarked. | via Alyssa Marie Alcantara/The FOREFRONT

# CHTM Sharks sealed the set against CEAS Mentors in Men’s Singles
[Image: CHTM Sharks sealed the set against CEAS Mentors in Men’s Singles]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**
TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—The College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks bagged the gold again during the men’s badminton singles championship match, 21-6, defeating the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors.
With his outstanding performance, Kryztofer Matias Basa of CHTM Sharks ended the finals with two straight sets, leaving no chance

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sealed-the-set-against-ceas-mentors-in-mens-singles]
nce, Kryztofer Matias Basa of CHTM Sharks ended the finals with two straight sets, leaving no chance for his opponent to recover.
In an interview, Basa shared that he already expected the victory as he had once played with a stronger player before.
“‘Yu‘n pa yung ginamit ko na motivation para magchampion ulit yung CHTM,” he said when asked about being pressured to defend the previous win of CHTM last SIKLAB.
He also expressed his joy in playing with passion and humility. “Iniisip ko lang is maging humble, and…sportsmanship,” he added.
Moreover, the College of Computer Studies secured the bronz

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sealed-the-set-against-ceas-mentors-in-mens-singles]
ng humble, and…sportsmanship,” he added.
Moreover, the College of Computer Studies secured the bronze medal, while the College of Business and Accountancy and the College of Allied Health got the 4th and 5th place, respectively. | via Jeliza Tadena/The FOREFRONT

# GCAAI Membership
#### Qualifications for Membership
A. The qualifications for membership to the Association are either:
1. Holder of academic degree from Gordon College (formerly Olongapo City Colleges)
2. Holder of secondary/basic education diploma from Gordon College-Senior High School
3. Holder of academic title or postgraduate diploma from Gordon College (formerly Olongapo City Colleges)
4. Not having been dismissed for misconduct/offenses or scholastic/academic deficiency which is automatically considered not a member of the Association.
B. Membership to the association may be bestowed

label: Gordon College
Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcaai/gcaai-membership]
 have paid and are up-to-date in their annual membership dues;
3. Members who have participated in such other programs that sustain, develop, and promote the Association’s objectives as determined by the Board.
D. The Alumni Oath shall be recited by a member who signifies its willingness to be part of and reflect its allegiance to the Association. The Alumni Oath shall be administered by the President or its duly authorized representative in a ceremony, commencement and/or event. The Alumni Oath in its current form is as follows:
*I **(state your name)**,*  
*a graduate of Gordon College,*  
*

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcaai/gcaai-membership]
 in its current form is as follows:
*I **(state your name)**,*  
*a graduate of Gordon College,*  
*hereby undertake to*  
*vigilantly upholds the ideals*  
*and shield the honor of my Alma Mater,*  
*and as a fulfilled member of the Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc.,*  
*will uphold its Constitution and By-Laws,*  
*to remain a true paragon towards excellence,*  
*committed to do justice to everyone*  
*in service to our country and our God.*  
*So help me, God!*

**Friday, October 17, 2025**
Due to the rising cases of Influenza-like illnesses, the City Health Office conducts disinfection procedures at 1:00pm, October 17, Gordon College Main and Annex Building.
With this, Face-to-face classes shifted to online mode in the afternoon and will resume the following day, October 18.

# Gordon College GAD Focal Point System Conducts Benchmarking Visit at PLM
[Image: Gordon College GAD Focal Point System Conducts Benchmarking Visit at PLM]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, August 11, 2025**
Representatives from various departments of Gordon College, serving as members of the Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System, successfully concluded a benchmarking visit to Pamantasan ng Lungsod ng Maynila (PLM) last August 08, 2025. The activity aimed to strengthen the College’s GAD programs by learning from PLM’s best practices in promoting gender

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-gad-focal-point-system-conducts-benchmarking-visit-at-plm]
d to strengthen the College’s GAD programs by learning from PLM’s best practices in promoting gender equality, inclusivity, and institutional development.
The delegation was warmly welcomed by PLM officials and staff. The visit featured comprehensive presentations on PLM’s Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) framework, gender-responsive policies, and student-centered initiatives. Delegates also toured PLM’s academic and administrative facilities, including specialized units dedicated to personnel and student support and welfare.
An open forum provided an opportunity for Gordon College repre

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-gad-focal-point-system-conducts-benchmarking-visit-at-plm]
nnel and student support and welfare.
An open forum provided an opportunity for Gordon College representatives from different units to engage with PLM counterparts, exchanging strategies on implementing GAD-related projects, integrating gender perspectives in policies.
This activity reinforces Gordon College’s commitment to fostering a gender-responsive, inclusive, and equitable academic environment through continuous learning and partnership with other higher education institutions.

# Responsibilities of the Data Subjects
Associated to the exercise of rights are responsibilities to respect the privacy of others and comply with Gordon College’s regulations in its administration of data privacy.
Data subjects shall have the following responsibilities:
1. Respect the data privacy rights of others;
2. Report any suspected security incident or personal data breach to Gordon College Data Protection Officer by sending an email or accomplishing OCP-DPU Form No. 4;
3. Provide Gordon College with true and accurate personal data and other information. Before submitting personal data

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/responsibilities-of-the-data-subjects]
 College with true and accurate personal data and other information. Before submitting personal data of different people to Gordon College, obtain the consent of such people.
4. Not disclose to any unauthorized party any non-public confidential, sensitive or personal information obtained or learned in confidence from Gordon College; and
5. Abide by the policies, guidelines and rules of Gordon College on data privacy, information security, records management, research and ethical conduct. From time to time, check for and comply with updates on these policies, guidelines and rules. Students may

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/responsibilities-of-the-data-subjects]
me to time, check for and comply with updates on these policies, guidelines and rules. Students may also refer to their Student Handbook.

# Temporary Easing of School Uniform Policy
[Image: Temporary Easing of School Uniform Policy]
**Tuesday, April 7, 2026**
𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓
In anticipation of 𝐞𝐱𝐭𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞 𝐡𝐞𝐚𝐭 𝐜𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 in the coming days, a 𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐬𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐨𝐥 𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦 𝐩𝐨𝐥𝐢𝐜𝐲 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐝.
Effective 𝐀𝐩𝐫𝐢𝐥 𝟏𝟑, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔 𝐨𝐧𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐬, students are allowed to wear civilian attire from Monday to Thursday upon entering the school premises, provided that they strictly adhere to the prescribed dress code.
Students are reminded to dress appropriately at all times.

# Galaxies of Unity: CCS Students Explore New Worlds Together
[Image: Galaxies of Unity: CCS Students Explore New Worlds Together]
[CCS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ccs/) [ForeFront](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/forefront/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, September 14, 2025**
ASINAN COVERED COURT, OLONGAPO CITY—The College of Computer Studies (CCS) of Gordon College launched its academic year with a universe of energy at the annual General Orientation and Acquaintance Party, “Galaxies of Unity: Exploring New Worlds Together,” held on September 13

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
ion and Acquaintance Party, “Galaxies of Unity: Exploring New Worlds Together,” held on September 13, 2025, at the Asinan Covered Court.
The event aims to transform into a constellation of colors, performances, advocacies, and camaraderie, welcoming both new and returning students into a community built on diversity, inclusion, and shared growth.
Furthermore, carrying the banner “Innovate, Include, Inspire: Pioneering Equality and Diversity in the AI-Driven World,” the orientation extended beyond the usual acquaintance party.
The event, spearheaded by Dean Ronnie Luy and Assistant Dean Armilyn

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
d the usual acquaintance party.
The event, spearheaded by Dean Ronnie Luy and Assistant Dean Armilyn Martinez with the support of the CCS faculty and coordinators, united students from the three CCS undergraduate programs—Bachelor of Science in Computer Science (BSCS), Bachelor of Science in Information Technology (BSIT), and Bachelor of Science in Entertainment and Multimedia Computing (BSEMC)—in celebrating inclusivity, teamwork, and talent.
“This event is not just for acquaintances, but also for orientation and presentation of our organizations and sportsfest participants. We want freshmen

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
for orientation and presentation of our organizations and sportsfest participants. We want freshmen to feel welcome and informed, and for everyone to know the CCS community they belong to,” shared Ms. Yheng Miel Sanchez, CCS faculty.
At the heart of the program was the introduction of eight planetary teams, each representing a social cause: Lumora for Accessibility and Compassion (Persons with Disabilities), Veltraxis for Collaboration (Ethnicity and Race), Aurelia for Empathy (Senior Citizens), Cryonex for Innovation (AI and Robotics), Floraxis for Sustainability and Culture (Cultural Heritag

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
 Cryonex for Innovation (AI and Robotics), Floraxis for Sustainability and Culture (Cultural Heritage), Kahelion for Resilience and Advocacy (Frontline Workers), Nyxara for Identity (LGBTQIA+), and Zenthara for Equity (Women Empowerment and Gender Equity).
Moreover, the day opened with an invocation, welcome remarks, and the general assembly before teams were formally revealed. Excitement built as each segment unfolded: cosplay presentations highlighted student creativity and confidence; production numbers filled the venue with music and dance; interactive games such as Constellation Connectio

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
ion numbers filled the venue with music and dance; interactive games such as Constellation Connection, Bring Me, Inclusion Mosaic, One Word Explains All, Voices of the Galaxy, and The Matrix sparked laughter and teamwork; entrepreneurial booths offered handmade crochets, stickers, badges, and keychains; Echoes of Unity, the team chant presentations, raised school spirit; and raffle draws added surprise and fun throughout the day.
“Okay lang naman yung event, nakakapagod pero enjoyable. Pinakagusto ko yung cosplay, because makikita mo yung confidence and effort ng mga sumali,” said Jerry Andrew

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
to ko yung cosplay, because makikita mo yung confidence and effort ng mga sumali,” said Jerry Andrew Famanila, CCS Student.
The cheers peaked with the introduction of players for CCS Sportsfest 2025, closing with the fiery chant, “Go CCS Phoenix!”. Student organizations, including the CCS Student Council, Society of Programming Enthusiasts in Computer Science (SPECS), Empowered League of Information Technology Education Students (ELITES), and Innovative Multimedia Artists Guild of Empowered Students (IMAGES), ensured the program’s smooth flow by managing venue setup, technical aspects, and coo

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
ents (IMAGES), ensured the program’s smooth flow by managing venue setup, technical aspects, and coordination.
Between lively intermissions, the spotlight turned to academic achievements. The Dean’s Lister Awarding Ceremony honored the Top 10 achievers per program (BSCS, BSIT, BSEMC–Digital Animation, BSEMC–Game Development) and the Top 50 overall Dean’s Listers for A.Y. 2024–2025. Special recognitions, including Top Cosplayers, Best Production Number, and Winning Team Planet, added a celebratory flair, affirming that CCS nurtures both artistry and innovation.
In addition, John Derick De Leon,

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
y flair, affirming that CCS nurtures both artistry and innovation.
In addition, John Derick De Leon, BSIT Student, shared that everything went according to the plan—which makes it very satisfying.
“Medyo mainit at may mga segment na biglaan pero doon lumalabas yung creativity ng students. Kitang-kita rin yung teamwork. Favorite ko yung part na bigla kaming pinag-drawing sa Inclusion Mosaic,” Mateo B. Bandilio, BSEMC student, shared.
Moreover, while the day brimmed with laughter, its deeper purpose was clear: to introduce freshmen to the CCS culture, strengthen bonds among students, and celebra

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
se was clear: to introduce freshmen to the CCS culture, strengthen bonds among students, and celebrate diversity as a source of strength. By merging advocacies with artistry and recognition with entertainment, the event became more than a kick-off celebration. It reaffirmed CCS’s mission to innovate, include, and inspire while promoting equality across differences.
As acknowledgments and final remarks drew the day to a close, ‘Galaxies of Unity’ stood as more than an acquaintance party. Guided by their advocacies, CCS students are set to carry the spirit of unity into the rest of the academic

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/galaxies-of-unity-ccs-students-explore-new-worlds-together]
y their advocacies, CCS students are set to carry the spirit of unity into the rest of the academic year, a reminder that while they may belong to different ‘planets’ of identity, talent, and cause, together they can form a universe of their own | Featured by Charyll Mae Cabungcal/The FOREFRONT

# CEAS Mentors, ibinida ang ginto sa Standard Category
[Image: CEAS Mentors, ibinida ang ginto sa Standard Category]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, September 15, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE, OLONGAPO CITY – Umangat ang kahusayan ng College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors sa taunang Dance Sports Competition Standard Category, matapos matagumpay na hinataw ang dalawang katunggali sa entablado sa pamamagitan ng tatlong klasikong sayaw.
Sinubukan ang kanilang husay at indak sa Waltz na nagpakita ng pino at walang-kupas na galaw, sa Tango na umigting sa m

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category]
ang husay at indak sa Waltz na nagpakita ng pino at walang-kupas na galaw, sa Tango na umigting sa matinding ekspresyon, at Quickstep na sumubok sa bilis, koordinasyon, at tibay ng bawat pares.
Sa kabila ng dikit na laban, naging kapansin-pansin ang stage presence, chemistry, kontrolado ngunit makapangyarihang galaw ni Jieann Cahanding at Ivan Russel Bahala mula sa CEAS Mentors na nagpakita ng malinis na footwork hanggang sa makabagbag na emosyon sa entablado. Sinundan ito ng CHTM (College of Hospitality and Tourism Management) Sharks sa ikalawang pwesto at CAHS (College of Allied and Health S

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category]
spitality and Tourism Management) Sharks sa ikalawang pwesto at CAHS (College of Allied and Health Studies) Tigers sa ikatlong pwesto.
“Aside from being student athletes, of course. Kami kasi ng partner ko first time partner, yung adjustment doon na tinuturuan ko siya kasi hindi rin siya talaga dance sport athlete, pero yung nakakamangha sa kaniya ay madali siya turuan, saad ni Jieann ukol sa hinarap nilang problema bago tumungtong sa entablado.
“Yung struggle namin ay ‘yong time management medyo mahirap kasi tuwing hapon lang kami nakakapag practice,” dagdag pa niya.
“Sobrang saya po na nakak

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category]
o mahirap kasi tuwing hapon lang kami nakakapag practice,” dagdag pa niya.
“Sobrang saya po na nakakaiyak. As a first-timer malaking challenge sa akin yun at malaking achievement,” ayon kay Bahala patungkol sa naramdaman matapos sungkitin ang kampeyonato.
Sa pagtatapos ng patimpalak, hindi lamang talento sa pagsasayaw ang naipakita ng mga kalahok. Bawat kolehiyo ay nagbigay ng kani-kaniyang tatak—mula sa makukulay na costume, malinis na footwork, hanggang sa mga koreograpiyang may hatid na emosyon at kwentong Gcians. | via Lianne Malazarte/The FOREFRONT

# GC receives COCPs of ALL of its undergraduate programs
[Image: GC receives COCPs of ALL of its undergraduate programs]
[Campus](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/campus/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, April 23, 2025**
In adherence to the directive of the Commission on Higher Education Regional Office III (CHEDRO III) mandating the conversion of Government Authority (GA) to Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC) for all academic programs, Gordon College is proud to announce that 100% of its undergraduate programs have successfully obtained COPC.
This

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-receives-cocps-of-all-of-its-undergraduate-programs]
 is proud to announce that 100% of its undergraduate programs have successfully obtained COPC.
This accomplishment would not have been possible without the unwavering support of Dr. Lora L. Yusi, Regional Director of CHEDRO III. We extend our heartfelt gratitude for her invaluable guidance throughout this journey.
We also express our deepest appreciation to Hon. Rolen C. Paulino, City Mayor; Hon. Cristabelle Marie C. Paulino, Chairperson of the Committee on Education; Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College President; Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs; the Board of Trustees Mem

# ONLINE Adding/Dropping of Classes
[Image: ONLINE Adding/Dropping of Classes]
**Monday, January 19, 2026**
**ANNOUNCEMENT:**
**ONLINE Adding/Dropping of Classes**
*Second Semester Academic Year 2025-2026*
**January 19-30, 2026**
[Image: ☑️]
Step 1
Log-on to the student portal ([https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gces/student](https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gces/student?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZWpzSlY3MHlQdVJWaXA0bnNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR5DCtJJZeE0IeMkAlOWYkAjlJ5HTa_uyuxL5M64lTq3dzJieHkPXJaQ0YalBg_aem_XfOZPmWrrx4p7jcYpDLqDg))
Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Ite

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/online-adding-dropping-of-classes-3]
5M64lTq3dzJieHkPXJaQ0YalBg_aem_XfOZPmWrrx4p7jcYpDLqDg))
Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Items.
[Image: ☑️]
Step 2
To request for Dropping of classes, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.
To request for adding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) on the search box. Click Add button of the desired class.
[Image: ☑️]
Step 3
Your selected subject(s) will appear under List of Subjects to Add/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.
Click submit to finalize your request or

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/online-adding-dropping-of-classes-3]
dd/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.
Click submit to finalize your request or cancel to clear selected classes.
[Image: ☑️]
Step 4
Your request will be evaluated by your program coordinator and will be verified by the college registrar.
Regularly visit the GCES portal for request updates.

# Soriano, naluklok na bagong BOD ng WUNI – Presidents
[Image: Soriano, naluklok na bagong BOD ng WUNI – Presidents]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, October 18, 2025**
Nahalal si Gordon College (GC) President Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano bilang bagong miyembro ng Board of Directors (BOD) ng prestihiyosong World University Networking for Innovation – Presidents (WUNI – Presidents) kasunod ng paglahok sa 2025 World University Rankings for Innovation (WURI).
Ang nasabing pagpupulong ay idinaos sa Tongmyong University, South Korea mula Setyembre 23 hanggang 25 ng kasal

label: Gordon College
content:

# CEAS Women’s Historic Smash Hit: Another Gold in Futsal Match
[Image: CEAS Women’s Historic Smash Hit: Another Gold in Futsal Match]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, September 19, 2025**
What a thrilling clash between pink and blue.
The College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors defeated College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks after a long showdown that ended in 3-0, and finished as champions in the Women’s Futsal match on the last day of SIKLAB 2025, held at New Asinan covered court.
The Mentors did not let their guard down eve

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-womens-historic-smash-hit-another-gold-in-futsal-match]
t day of SIKLAB 2025, held at New Asinan covered court.
The Mentors did not let their guard down ever since their first game, making sure everything is coordinated to one another until the final match.
Cheers roared through the court as the intensifying moments of the game went on, with Mentors maintaining their momentum throughout the match.
Despite the Sharks’ efforts to lessen the pressure, CEAS women clocked the game with a winning score of 3-0. | via Mariah Llave/The FOREFRONT

# Ballo and GCians Capture 1st and 2nd Runner-ups at Cultural Competition
[Image: Ballo and GCians Capture 1st and 2nd Runner-ups at Cultural Competition]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, December 23, 2025**
Gordon College’s (GC) Performing Arts Students Ensemble (PASE) Ballo and other students secured the 1st and 2nd runner-up titles in the Barangay Cultural Competition at Gordon Avenue, on December 20, at SM City Downtown.
Members of PASE Ballo and other GCians competed in their respective groups, representing Barangay Pag-asa and Barangay West Tapinac.
Baran

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ballo-and-gcians-capture-1st-and-2nd-runner-ups-at-cultural-competition]
 competed in their respective groups, representing Barangay Pag-asa and Barangay West Tapinac.
Barangay Pag-asa performed their Sagudsod de Mano, which led them to be placed as the first runner-up.
Meanwhile, Barangay West Tapinac earned their place as the second runner-up after grooving their way through their Inalimango performance.
Despite the wet roads due to the rain, the participants from the institution still showed their grace and effort in their renditions, embracing the Filipino culture.
The folk dance competition was a part of the Olongapo City Fiesta Celebration. | via Jasmine Zeah

# GC, Included in the World University Rankings for Innovation (WURI) 2025
[Image: GC, Included in the World University Rankings for Innovation (WURI) 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, July 11, 2025**
With immense pride and honor, Gordon College proudly shares its inclusion in the World University Rankings for Innovation (WURI) 2025: Crisis Management category, ranking in the Top 72. This milestone marks a significant achievement in our ongoing internationalization initiatives.
